root_motivation: Concentrate all full-dataset materialization in a single audited, bounded operation so that 'data crossed the boundary' is always a deliberate logged event — not a side effect of a summary or op — keeping the LLM-as-calculator guarantee honest.
user_persona: MCP server engineer or operator consuming the dh-* fleet; needs a sanctioned path to produce actual rows for the 'download the results' case without opening an unaudited spill path elsewhere.

acceptance_criteria.description: CSV export of a fixture handle produces correct, well-formed CSV (header + rows match dataset); round-trip parse equals source.
JSON export honors max_rows: dataset larger than max_rows is refused with typed error unless explicit override is set; within bound emits exactly the rows.
ExportReceipt reports correct row_count, bytes, and stable sha256 of payload (test asserts hash for fixed fixture).
File export writes atomically (no partial file on simulated mid-write failure) and refuses to overwrite existing file without overwrite flag.
Parquet export (behind parquet feature) produces file readable back to same rows; test is feature-gated and skipped cleanly when feature is off.
Exporting an expired/unknown handle returns a typed error, never a panic.
cargo test --release passes (default features); cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings clean.

scope: Single export() entry point: store+handle+fmt+dest+opts → ExportReceipt
Formats: CSV (always), JSON bounded by max_rows (always), Parquet (feature-gated)
Destinations: File (atomic tempfile+rename) and Inline (bounded by max_bytes)
ExportReceipt audit record: handle, fmt, dest, row_count, bytes, sha256, ts
Typed error enum covering all failure modes: LookupFailed, JsonLimitExceeded, InlineLimitExceeded, FileExists, Io, ParquetNotEnabled
non_goals: Streaming / chunked export (all-at-once only in v1)
Network destinations (S3, HTTP, etc.) — file or inline only
Audit log persistence — ExportReceipt is returned to caller; crate does not write a log itself
Multiple concurrent exports of the same handle — no locking on top of Store
Compression (gzip, zstd) on top of formats

additional.parquet_feature_optional: true — arrow+parquet behind feature flag; default build must be light

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a separate dh-export crate instead of adding export() to dh-store?
five_whys_trace.a: dh-store owns the in-memory representation; adding format serialization bloats it with CSV/JSON/Parquet deps and muddies the single responsibility. Export is a boundary concern, not a storage concern.

five_whys_trace.q: Why make this a hard boundary at all rather than letting any tool serialize a Dataset?
five_whys_trace.a: If any code path can spill rows, the LLM-as-calculator guarantee (MQO server: raw SQL is a rejected path) leaks silently. Concentrating materialization here makes auditing trivially complete: if ExportReceipt exists, data crossed; if not, it didn't.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the JSON exit be bounded by max_rows rather than relying on callers to pass small datasets?
five_whys_trace.a: Callers will forget. A million-row inline JSON dump is a latency and memory bomb in an MCP response. The type system should make the wrong thing require extra effort (override flag), not the right thing.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should ExportReceipt include the inline payload bytes directly?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes — inline_payload: Option<Vec<u8>>, Some only for Inline dest. File dest records the path in dest field; bytes live on disk. Serde: skip_serializing_if = None.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is 'atomic' on non-POSIX platforms in scope?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: POSIX only (macOS/Linux). tempfile::Builder + persist() gives the rename guarantee on POSIX. Windows is a non-goal for v1.
